name: Mrs. Owen's Cook Book Chili (1880)
recipeIngredient: lean beef - cut into small dice
recipeInstructions:
This may be the earliest printed recipe for chili con carne and it is surprisingly authentic, save for the suspect addition of "Espagnole" sauce. The words are Mrs. Owen's own.
Take lean beef and cut into small dice. Cook with a little oil until well braised. Add onions and garlic (chopped finely). Stir in flour, then add water or stock and continue cooking. Mix in two tablespoons of Espagnole sauce and season with 1 teaspoon each of ground oregano, cumin, and coriander.
Prepare the dried whole peppers by removing seeds and covering with water. Boil until cooked through and strain. Add enough puree to the stew to make it hot and salty to taste.
Serve with a border of Mexican beans (frijoles) that have been well cooked in salted water. Fry the beans in very hot lard until they are dry and light brown. Occasionally, chopped onions or red pepper pods may be added to the lard before the beans are incorporated.
